Package nonprofitbookkeeping.model

Class CurrentCompany.CompanyListener

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.model.CurrentCompany.CompanyListener
Enclosing class:
CurrentCompany
public static class CurrentCompany.CompanyListener extends Object
Manages a list of CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListeners and fires events to them.

    Description

    Method
    Description

    addCompanyListener(CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener l)
    Adds a CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener to the listener list.

    Notifies all registered CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListeners that the company open state changed.

    Returns the list of currently registered CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListeners.

  • Constructor Details

    • CompanyListener

      public CompanyListener()

  • Method Details

    • addCompanyListener

      public static void addCompanyListener(CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener l)
      Adds a CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener to the listener list.
      Parameters:
      l - The listener to add.

    • removeCompanyListener

      public static void removeCompanyListener(CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener l)
      Removes a CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener from the listener list.
      Parameters:
      l - The listener to remove.

    • getListeners

      public static List<CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListener> getListeners()
      Returns the list of currently registered CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListeners.

      This method exposes the listeners primarily for testing purposes so that UI tests can manually trigger company change events. The returned list is a snapshot – modifications to it will not affect the internal listener registry.

      Returns:
      an immutable List of registered listeners, or an empty list if none are registered

    • fireChanged

      public static void fireChanged(boolean isOpen)
      Notifies all registered CurrentCompany.CompanyChangeListeners that the company open state changed.
      Parameters:
      isOpen - true if the company is now open, false otherwise.
